pageTitle: Right Triangle Side Calculator - Solve Any Side or Hypotenuse | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Enter any two sides of a right triangle and the third — including the hypotenuse — is calculated automatically. The diagram updates to match your actual ratio, and you can verify a square corner against a real measurement.
softwareAppDescription: A free tool that uses the Pythagorean theorem to solve for the missing side of a right triangle from any two known sides, with a diagram that reflects the actual ratio you enter. Also checks a measured diagonal against the ideal for verifying square corners.
subtitle: Enter any two sides and the third is calculated automatically
taglineExtra: Whichever two sides you enter, the third is calculated instantly, and the triangle diagram is drawn to match your actual ratio.
setupIntro: Enter values for any two of the triangle's three sides, and the third is calculated automatically using the Pythagorean theorem. Side A is the horizontal side (base) in the diagram below, and Side B is the vertical side (height). Change a value and both the calculation and the diagram's ratio update to match.
invalidNumberError: Please enter a value greater than 0.
invalidTriangleError: The hypotenuse must be longer than both other sides for a right triangle to exist. Please check your values.
verifyIntro: Mark out the two sides on-site, measure the actual diagonal, and enter it below to see exactly how far off it is from the calculated hypotenuse above.
actualDiagonalPlaceholder: The diagonal length you actually measured
verifyPassTemplate: ✓ This is very close to a true right angle (off by {diff})
verifyFailTemplate: ✗ This differs from a true right angle (off by {diff})
invalidActualDiagonalError: Please enter a measured diagonal length greater than 0.
verifyNeedsHypotenuseMessage: The hypotenuse needs to be calculated first before you can verify against it.
infoIntroH2: What is this right triangle calculator?
infoIntroP: This calculator uses the Pythagorean theorem (sideA² + sideB² = hypotenuse²) to instantly solve for the missing side of a right triangle from any two known values. Enter Side A and Side B and it solves for the hypotenuse (diagonal); enter the hypotenuse and one side and it solves for the other side. The classic "3-4-5 rule" used in carpentry, framing, landscaping, and deck building works perfectly here too, but you're not limited to it — plug in whatever real measurements you have. Change any value and the diagram below redraws to match your actual ratio.
infoHowtoP: Enter any two of Side A, Side B, and the Hypotenuse — the remaining field fills in automatically. If all three are already filled and you enter a new value somewhere, the field you filled in longest ago gets recalculated and updated instead. Mark out the two sides on-site using the calculated hypotenuse, measure the actual diagonal, and enter it in the "On-site verification" field to see how far off it is from the ideal and whether the corner is square.
infoUseCases: Quickly checking whether a wall or floor corner is square without a framing square or protractor
Squaring up baselines for deck, tile, or landscaping projects
Working out a diagonal length in advance for furniture or woodworking frames
Quickly back-calculating a missing side from two measurements you already have

faq.q: Do I have to use a 3:4:5 ratio?
faq.a: No. Any two values that form a right triangle will work — 3:4:5 is just the most well-known example, and this calculator works for any ratio.

faq.q: What happens if I enter a new value when all three are already filled?
faq.a: Type into any of the three fields and the field you filled in longest ago gets recalculated and updated automatically.

faq.q: What's the tolerance for the on-site verification check?
faq.a: A difference within about 0.5% of the ideal hypotenuse length is treated as "very close to a true right angle" — a margin that accounts for typical tape-measure accuracy on a job site.

privacyNote: This tool performs all calculations entirely in your browser. Nothing you enter is sent to or stored on a server.
